hardBlocks: Specific Emmy claim ('19 Emmy nominations, winning three for visual effects and sound editing') is precise enough to be a likely fabrication risk — BSG's Emmy tally was largely in technical categories but the exact figures need verification; presented as fact without sourcing.

strengths: Strong BLUF lead that names the #1 pick and justifies it immediately, opening a loop on 'why'.
Clean structure: multiple question H2s, key takeaways, numbered ranking, robust 6-FAQ block with accurate, specific answers.
Confident, specific voice with no AI filler; rewatchability and 'where to start' angles add genuine reader benefit.
Verifiable claims hold up: BSG Peabody Award, Time 100 list, Moore's episode podcast, The Expanse 2015–2022, Severance premise, Westworld four seasons (2016–2022).
weaknesses: External sources are mostly streaming/Wikipedia landing pages, not authoritative critical/journalistic sources (e.g., no Rolling Stone, IGN, NYT, Peabody.org) — weakens E-E-A-T claim of '3 authoritative external sources'.
No internal links present at all, a required element for rank-#1 standard.
Primary keyword 'best sci-fi tv shows' appears in title/H2 but could be seeded more naturally in the lead and meta; no explicit meta description provided.
Ranking is thin at only 5 entries for an 'of all time' list — omits acknowledged influences (Star Trek, The Twilight Zone, Doctor Who, X-Files) that readers will expect, hurting completeness.
No table or comparison element (platform/seasons/run-years) where it would aid scannability.
